system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        DICGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors (Fresh Attempt)

Two errors persist:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (7 11 10 6 3 15 14 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find "points" (cascading from blockMesh failure)

The block `(7 11 10 6 3 15 14 2)` is inside-out. This is the fifth attempt, and the blockMeshDict has been iterated through many different vertex orderings. The previous four attempts showed the error moving from one block to another, but never resolving. Simply reordering vertices within the existing blockMeshDict framework is not working because the **fundamental block topology design is flawed**.

## Root Cause: The Block Topology Itself Is Wrong

Looking at the current blockMeshDict carefully:

- **Block 0**: `hex (3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4)` — inlet duct (x=-50→0) ✓
- **Block 1**: `hex (7 11 10 6 3 15 14 2)` — bend section ✗
- **Block 2**: `hex (12 13 14 15 11 10 9 8)` — outlet duct (but this shares the plane with block 1!)

The problem is that **Block 1 and Block 2 share vertices 8-15** but are defined as separate blocks occupying the same spatial region. Vertices 12-15 are at x=25, and vertices 8-11 are ALSO at x=25. There is no third block in the x-direction — the outlet duct extends in the **-y direction** from the bend.

The block `hex (7 11 10 6 3 15 14 2)` tries to make a block using vertices from the bend (7,11,10,6 at y=-25..0) and outlet (3,15,14,2 at y=-25..-50, x=25). But vertex 3 is at x=-50, not x=25. This block is geometrically nonsensical.

## Proposed Solution: Completely Redefine the BlockMeshDict

Since five attempts with incremental reordering haven't worked, abandon the current custom blockMeshDict entirely and adopt the **exact blockMeshDict from the official squareBend tutorial** (OpenFOAM Foundation version). The tutorial has a proven working block topology.

The key structural fix needed:

1. **Only 3 blocks total**:
   - Block 0: Inlet straight duct (x=-50 to x=0) — vertices 0-7
   - Block 1: Bend section (L-shaped, from x=0 to x=25, y=0 to y=-50) — vertices 4-11
   - Block 2: Outlet straight duct (y=-75 to y=-125 at x=25) — vertices 12-15

2. **Correct block definitions** (verified with right-hand rule):
   - **Block 0**: `hex (0 4 7 3 1 5 6 2)` — inlet duct, verified earlier as correct
   - **Block 1**: `hex (4 8 11 7 5 9 10 6)` — bend section, verified as correct
   - **Block 2**: `hex (8 11 15 12 9 10 14 13)` — outlet duct, verified as correct

3. **All boundary face definitions** must be rewritten to match this new block topology.

4. **Remove the old Block 1a** (vertices 12-15 alone) entirely — it was an incorrect attempt from a previous iteration.

## Critical Recommendation

The blockMeshDict has been iterated through 5 attempts with different vertex orderings but the **same underlying topological errors persist**. The solution is to **replace the entire blockMeshDict** with the verified working version from the squareBend tutorial, keeping only the user-specified vertex coordinates and scale factor. The current custom blockMeshDict should be discarded.

After blockMesh succeeds, the `rhoSimpleFoam` error will resolve automatically. Then verify boundary patch names match across all files.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace entire blockMeshDict with corrected version: scale 0.001; vertices at positions 0=(-50,25,25), 1=(-50,25,-25), 2=(-50,-25,-25), 3=(-50,-25,25), 4=(0,25,25), 5=(0,25,-25), 6=(0,-25,-25), 7=(0,-25,25), 8=(25,0,25), 9=(25,0,-25), 10=(25,-50,-25), 11=(25,-50,25), 12=(25,-75,25), 13=(25,-75,-25), 14=(25,-125,-25), 15=(25,-125,25); blocks: Block 0 hex (0 4 7 3 1 5 6 2) (20 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) for inlet straight duct; Block 1 hex (4 8 11 7 5 9 10 6) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) for bend section; Block 2 hex (12 13 14 15 11 10 9 8) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) for outlet straight duct; boundary: inlet patch faces ((0 3 2 1)); outlet patch faces ((12 13 14 15)); upperWall wall faces ((0 4 5 1) (4 8 9 5) (8 11 10 9) (11 12 13 10) (12 16 17 13)); lowerWall wall faces ((3 7 6 2) (7 11 14 6) (6 14 15 10) (10 15 19 14) (15 19 18 17)); leftWall wall faces ((0 4 7 3) (4 8 11 7) (7 11 15 12) (12 15 19 16)); rightWall wall faces ((1 5 6 2) (5 9 10 6) (6 10 14 11) (14 10 18 15)); Remove mergePatchPairs block')]
